1064		// ExternalTrafficPolicyLocal preserves the source IP of the traffic by
1065		// routing only to endpoints on the same node as the traffic was received on
1066		// (dropping the traffic if there are no local endpoints).
1067		ExternalTrafficPolicyLocal ExternalTrafficPolicy = ExternalTrafficPolicy(corev1.ServiceExternalTrafficPolicyLocal)
1068	)
1069	
1070	// NodePort creates a port on each node on which the NGINX data plane service is exposed. The NodePort MUST
1071	// map to a Gateway listener port, otherwise it will be ignored. If not specified, Kubernetes allocates a NodePort
1072	// automatically if required. The default NodePort range enforced by Kubernetes is 30000-32767.
1073	type NodePort struct {
1074		// Port is the NodePort to expose.
1075		// +kubebuilder:validation:Minimum=1
1076		// +kubebuilder:validation:Maximum=65535
1077		Port int32 `json:"port"`
1078	
1079		// ListenerPort is the Gateway listener port that this NodePort maps to.
1080		// +kubebuilder:validation:Minimum=1
1081		// +kubebuilder:validation:Maximum=65535
1082		ListenerPort int32 `json:"listenerPort"`
1083	}
1084	
1085	// HostPort exposes an nginx container port on the host.
1086	type HostPort struct {
1087		// Port to expose on the host.
1088		// +kubebuilder:validation:Minimum=1
1089		// +kubebuilder:validation:Maximum=65535
1090		Port int32 `json:"port"`
1091	
1092		// ContainerPort is the port on the nginx container to map to the HostPort.
1093		// +kubebuilder:validation:Minimum=1
1094		// +kubebuilder:validation:Maximum=65535
1095		ContainerPort int32 `json:"containerPort"`
1096	}
1097	
1098	// Compression defines the configuration for HTTP response compression.
1099	// +kubebuilder:validation:XValidation:message="type 'gzip' requires spec.compression.gzip to be set",rule="!(self.type == 'gzip' && !has(self.gzip))"
1100	//
1101	//nolint:lll
1102	type Compression struct {
1103		// Gzip defines gzip module-specific compression settings.
1104		//
1105		// +optional
1106		Gzip *GzipSettings `json:"gzip,omitempty"`
1107		// Buffers sets the number and size of buffers used to compress a response.
1108		//
1109		// NGINX directive: https://nginx.org/en/docs/http/ngx_http_gzip_module.html#gzip_buffers
1110		//
1111		// +optional
1112		Buffers *CompressionBuffers `json:"buffers,omitempty"`
1113		// Level sets the compression level.
1114		// Higher values provide better compression but use more CPU.
1115		//
1116		// NGINX directive: https://nginx.org/en/docs/http/ngx_http_gzip_module.html#gzip_comp_level
1117		//
1118		// +optional
1119		// +kubebuilder:validation:Minimum=1
1120		// +kubebuilder:validation:Maximum=9
1121		Level *int32 `json:"level,omitempty"`
1122		// MinLength sets the minimum length of a response that will be compressed.
1123		// The length is determined from the "Content-Length" response header field.
1124		//
1125		// NGINX directive: https://nginx.org/en/docs/http/ngx_http_gzip_module.html#gzip_min_length
1126		//
1127		// +optional
1128		// +kubebuilder:validation:Minimum=0
1129		MinLength *int32 `json:"minLength,omitempty"`
1130		// Type specifies the compression algorithm to use.
1131		// Currently only gzip is supported.
1132		//
1133		Type CompressionType `json:"type"`
1134		// MimeTypes specifies the MIME types to compress in addition to "text/html".
1135		// "text/html" is always compressed when compression is enabled.
1136		// Wildcards like "text/*" are not supported by NGINX.
1137		// Example: ["application/json", "text/css", "application/javascript"]
1138		//
1139		// NGINX directive: https://nginx.org/en/docs/http/ngx_http_gzip_module.html#gzip_types
1140		//
1141		// +optional
1142		// +kubebuilder:validation:MinItems=1
1143		// +kubebuilder:validation:MaxItems=32
1144		MimeTypes []string `json:"mimeTypes,omitempty"`
1145	}
1146	
1147	// CompressionBuffers defines the number and size of buffers used for compression.
1148	type CompressionBuffers struct {
1149		// Size sets the size of each buffer.
1150		Size v1alpha1.Size `json:"size"`
1151	
1152		// Number sets the number of buffers.
1153		//
1154		// +kubebuilder:validation:Minimum=1
1155		// +kubebuilder:validation:Maximum=256
1156		Number int32 `json:"number"`
1157	}
1158	
1159	// CompressionType defines the type of compression algorithm.
1160	// +kubebuilder:validation:Enum=gzip
1161	type CompressionType string
1162	
1163	const (
1164		// GzipCompressionType specifies gzip compression.
1165		GzipCompressionType CompressionType = "gzip"
1166	)
1167	
1168	// GzipSettings defines gzip module-specific compression settings.
1169	type GzipSettings struct {
1170		// Vary enables or disables inserting the "Vary: Accept-Encoding" response header
1171		// when gzip compression is active.
1172		//
1173		// NGINX directive: https://nginx.org/en/docs/http/ngx_http_gzip_module.html#gzip_vary
1174		//
1175		// +optional
1176		Vary *bool `json:"vary,omitempty"`
1177		// HTTPVersion sets the minimum HTTP version of a request required to compress a response.
1178		//
1179		// NGINX directive: https://nginx.org/en/docs/http/ngx_http_gzip_module.html#gzip_http_version
1180		//
1181		// +optional
1182		HTTPVersion *GzipHTTPVersion `json:"httpVersion,omitempty"`
1183		// Disable specifies regular expressions to match User-Agent headers of requests
1184		// that should not be gzip-compressed.
1185		//
1186		// NGINX directive: https://nginx.org/en/docs/http/ngx_http_gzip_module.html#gzip_disable
1187		//
1188		// +optional
1189		// +kubebuilder:validation:MinItems=1
1190		// +kubebuilder:validation:MaxItems=16
1191		Disable []string `json:"disable,omitempty"`
1192		// Proxied enables or disables gzip compression for proxied requests depending on the request and response.
1193		// Accepted values are: "off", "expired", "no-cache", "no-store", "private", "no_last_modified",
1194		// "no_etag", "auth", "any".
1195		// Multiple values can be specified.
1196		//
1197		// NGINX directive: https://nginx.org/en/docs/http/ngx_http_gzip_module.html#gzip_proxied
1198		//
1199		// +optional
1200		// +kubebuilder:validation:MinItems=1
1201		// +kubebuilder:validation:MaxItems=9
1202		Proxied []GzipProxiedType `json:"proxied,omitempty"`
1203	}
1204	
1205	// GzipProxiedType defines the conditions under which responses from proxied requests are compressed.
1206	// +kubebuilder:validation:Enum=off;expired;no-cache;no-store;private;no_last_modified;no_etag;auth;any
1207	type GzipProxiedType string
1208	
1209	const (
1210		// GzipProxiedOff disables compression for all proxied requests.
1211		GzipProxiedOff GzipProxiedType = "off"
1212		// GzipProxiedExpired enables compression if a response header includes the "Expires" field.
1213		GzipProxiedExpired GzipProxiedType = "expired"
1214		// GzipProxiedNoCache enables compression if a response header includes
1215		// "Cache-Control" with the "no-cache" parameter.
1216		GzipProxiedNoCache GzipProxiedType = "no-cache"
1217		// GzipProxiedNoStore enables compression if a response header includes
1218		// "Cache-Control" with the "no-store" parameter.
1219		GzipProxiedNoStore GzipProxiedType = "no-store"
1220		// GzipProxiedPrivate enables compression if a response header includes
1221		// "Cache-Control" with the "private" parameter.
1222		GzipProxiedPrivate GzipProxiedType = "private"
1223		// GzipProxiedNoLastModified enables compression if a response header does not include "Last-Modified".
1224		GzipProxiedNoLastModified GzipProxiedType = "no_last_modified"
1225		// GzipProxiedNoETag enables compression if a response header does not include "ETag".
1226		GzipProxiedNoETag GzipProxiedType = "no_etag"
1227		// GzipProxiedAuth enables compression if a request header includes "Authorization".
1228		GzipProxiedAuth GzipProxiedType = "auth"
1229		// GzipProxiedAny enables compression for all proxied requests.
1230		GzipProxiedAny GzipProxiedType = "any"
1231	)
1232	
1233	// GzipHTTPVersion defines the minimum HTTP version required for gzip compression.
1234	// +kubebuilder:validation:Enum="1.0";"1.1"
1235	type GzipHTTPVersion string
1236	
1237	const (
1238		// GzipHTTPVersion10 sets the minimum HTTP version to 1.0.
1239		GzipHTTPVersion10 GzipHTTPVersion = "1.0"
1240		// GzipHTTPVersion11 sets the minimum HTTP version to 1.1.
1241		GzipHTTPVersion11 GzipHTTPVersion = "1.1"
1242	)

content.content:
1	package graph
2	
3	import (
4		"bytes"
5		"context"
6		"errors"
7		"fmt"
8		"slices"
9		"sort"
10		"strings"
11	
12		"github.com/go-logr/logr"
13		corev1 "k8s.io/api/core/v1"
14		"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/apis/meta/v1/unstructured"
15		"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime/schema"
16		"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/types"
17		"sigs.k8s.io/controller-runtime/pkg/client"
18		v1 "sigs.k8s.io/gateway-api/apis/v1"
19	
20		ngfAPIv1alpha1 "github.com/nginx/nginx-gateway-fabric/v2/apis/v1alpha1"
21		wafv1 "github.com/nginx/nginx-gateway-fabric/v2/apis/waf/v1"
22		"github.com/nginx/nginx-gateway-fabric/v2/internal/controller/ngfsort"
23		"github.com/nginx/nginx-gateway-fabric/v2/internal/controller/nginx/config/policies"
24		"github.com/nginx/nginx-gateway-fabric/v2/internal/controller/state/conditions"
25		"github.com/nginx/nginx-gateway-fabric/v2/internal/controller/state/graph/shared/secrets"
26		"github.com/nginx/nginx-gateway-fabric/v2/internal/controller/state/validation"
27		"github.com/nginx/nginx-gateway-fabric/v2/internal/framework/helpers"
28		"github.com/nginx/nginx-gateway-fabric/v2/internal/framework/kinds"
29		"github.com/nginx/nginx-gateway-fabric/v2/internal/framework/waf/fetch"
30		s3fetch "github.com/nginx/nginx-gateway-fabric/v2/internal/framework/waf/fetch/s3"
31	)
32	
33	// Policy represents an NGF Policy.
34	type Policy struct {
35		// Source is the corresponding Policy resource.
36		Source policies.Policy
37		// InvalidForGateways is a map of Gateways for which this Policy is invalid for. Certain NginxProxy
38		// configurations may result in a policy not being valid for some Gateways, but not others.
39		// This includes gateways that cannot accept the policy due to ancestor status limits.
40		InvalidForGateways map[types.NamespacedName]struct{}
41		// WAFState holds WAF-specific state for this policy. Only populated for WAFPolicy resources.
42		WAFState *PolicyWAFState
43		// Ancestors is a list of ancestor objects of the Policy. Used in status.
44		Ancestors []PolicyAncestor
45		// TargetRefs are the resources that the Policy targets.
46		TargetRefs []PolicyTargetRef
47		// Conditions holds the conditions for the Policy.
48		// These conditions apply to the entire Policy.
49		// The conditions in the Ancestor apply only to the Policy in regard to the Ancestor.
50		Conditions []conditions.Condition
51		// Valid indicates whether the Policy is valid.
52		Valid bool
53	}
54	
55	// PolicyWAFState holds WAF-specific state for a Policy.
56	// This is only populated for WAFPolicy resources.
57	type PolicyWAFState struct {
58		// Bundles contains the fetched WAF bundle data for this policy.
59		// This allows each gateway to receive only the bundles for policies that target it.
60		Bundles map[WAFBundleKey]*WAFBundleData
61		// ResolvedAuth contains the resolved authentication credentials for WAF bundle fetching.
62		// Stored so that the WAF polling manager can re-use them without re-resolving secrets.
63		ResolvedAuth *fetch.BundleAuth
64		// ResolvedTLSCA contains the resolved TLS CA certificate data for WAF bundle fetching.
65		ResolvedTLSCA []byte
66		// BundlePending is true when the policy's bundle has never been successfully fetched
67		// (cold-miss on startup or after all retries are exhausted with no previous bundle).
68		// The Gateway config push is withheld until this is resolved to maintain fail-closed posture.
69		BundlePending bool
70	}
71	
72	// PolicyAncestor represents an ancestor of a Policy.
73	type PolicyAncestor struct {
74		// Ancestor is the ancestor object.
75		Ancestor v1.ParentReference
76		// Conditions contains the list of conditions of the Policy in relation to the ancestor.
77		Conditions []conditions.Condition
78	}
79	
80	// PolicyTargetRef represents the object that the Policy is targeting.
81	type PolicyTargetRef struct {
82		// Kind is the Kind of the object.
83		Kind v1.Kind
84		// Group is the Group of the object.
85		Group v1.Group
86		// Nsname is the NamespacedName of the object.
87		Nsname types.NamespacedName
88	}
89	
90	// PolicyKey is a unique identifier for an NGF Policy.
91	type PolicyKey struct {
92		// Nsname is the NamespacedName of the Policy.
93		NsName types.NamespacedName
94		// GVK is the GroupVersionKind of the Policy.
95		GVK schema.GroupVersionKind
96	}
97	
98	// WAFBundleKey uniquely identifies a WAF bundle on disk.
99	// Format: "<namespace>_<policyName>" for policy bundles, or "<namespace>_<policyName>_log_<urlHash>" for log bundles,
100	// where urlHash is a truncated SHA-256 hex digest of the log source URL.
101	type WAFBundleKey string
102	
103	// PolicyBundleKey returns the WAFBundleKey for a WAFPolicy's main policy bundle.
104	func PolicyBundleKey(policyNsName types.NamespacedName) WAFBundleKey {
105		return WAFBundleKey(fmt.Sprintf("%s_%s", policyNsName.Namespace, policyNsName.Name))
106	}
107	
108	// LogBundleKey returns the WAFBundleKey for a SecurityLog entry's bundle.
109	// The key for NIM is formatted as "<namespace>_<policyName>_log_<nimUrlHash>_<nimProfileName>", where nimUrlHash is a truncated SHA-256 hex digest of the NIM instance URL.
110	// The key for N1C is formatted as "<namespace>_<policyName>_log_<n1cUrlHash>_<n1cNamespace>_<n1cProfileIdentifier>", where n1cUrlHash is a truncated SHA-256 hex digest of the N1C instance URL, and n1cProfileIdentifier is either the ProfileObjectID or ProfileName (if ObjectID is not set).
111	// The key for HTTP is formatted as "<namespace>_<policyName>_log_<httpUrlHash>", where httpUrlHash is a truncated SHA-256 hex digest of the HTTP URL.
112	//
113	//nolint:lll
114	func LogBundleKey(policyNsName types.NamespacedName, logSource *ngfAPIv1alpha1.LogSource) WAFBundleKey {
115		if logSource == nil {
116			return ""
117		}
118		if logSource.NIMSource != nil && logSource.NIMSource.URL != "" {
119			return WAFBundleKey(
120				fmt.Sprintf(
121					"%s_%s_log_%s_%s",
122					policyNsName.Namespace, policyNsName.Name,
123					helpers.URLHash(logSource.NIMSource.URL),
124					logSource.NIMSource.ProfileName,
125				),
126			)
127		}
128	
129		if logSource.N1CSource != nil {
130			profileIdentifier := ""
131			if logSource.N1CSource.ProfileObjectID != nil {
132				profileIdentifier = *logSource.N1CSource.ProfileObjectID
133			} else if logSource.N1CSource.ProfileName != nil {
134				profileIdentifier = *logSource.N1CSource.ProfileName
135			}
136			return WAFBundleKey(
137				fmt.Sprintf(
138					"%s_%s_log_%s_%s_%s",
139					policyNsName.Namespace, policyNsName.Name,
140					helpers.URLHash(logSource.N1CSource.URL),
141					logSource.N1CSource.Namespace,
142					profileIdentifier,
143				),
144			)
145		}
146	
147		if logSource.HTTPSource == nil {
148			return ""
149		}
150		return WAFBundleKey(
151			fmt.Sprintf("%s_%s_log_%s", policyNsName.Namespace, policyNsName.Name, helpers.URLHash(logSource.HTTPSource.URL)),
152		)
153	}
154	
155	// LogBundleDescription returns a human-readable label for a log profile bundle source.
156	// Used in status condition messages to identify which bundle is being reported on.
157	// The switch uses boolean conditions rather than a type switch because LogSource carries
158	// optional pointer fields — only one of NIMSource, N1CSource, or HTTPSource will be set.
159	func LogBundleDescription(src *ngfAPIv1alpha1.LogSource) string {
160		if src == nil {
161			return "security log bundle"
162		}
163		switch {
164		case src.NIMSource != nil:
165			return fmt.Sprintf("security log bundle (profile: %s)", src.NIMSource.ProfileName)
166		case src.N1CSource != nil:
167			if src.N1CSource.ProfileName != nil {
168				return fmt.Sprintf("security log bundle (profile: %s)", *src.N1CSource.ProfileName)
169			}
170			if src.N1CSource.ProfileObjectID != nil {
171				return fmt.Sprintf("security log bundle (profile: %s)", *src.N1CSource.ProfileObjectID)
172			}
173			return "security log bundle"
174		case src.HTTPSource != nil:
175			return fmt.Sprintf("security log bundle (URL: %s)", src.HTTPSource.URL)
176		default:
177			return "security log bundle"
178		}
179	}
180	
181	// WAFBundleData contains the fetched WAF bundle content.
182	type WAFBundleData struct {
183		Checksum string
184		Data     []byte
185	}
186	
187	// PLMRole identifies the role of a Kubernetes Secret in PLM S3 storage authentication.
188	type PLMRole string
189	
190	const (
191		// PLMRoleCredentials is the role for the Secret containing S3 access key ID and secret access key.
192		PLMRoleCredentials PLMRole = "credentials"
193		// PLMRoleCA is the role for the Secret containing the CA certificate for TLS verification.
194		PLMRoleCA PLMRole = "ca"
195		// PLMRoleClientSSL is the role for the Secret containing the client TLS certificate and key for mTLS.
196		PLMRoleClientSSL PLMRole = "clientssl"
197	)
198	
199	const (
200		gatewayGroupKind = v1.GroupName + "/" + kinds.Gateway
201		hrGroupKind      = v1.GroupName + "/" + kinds.HTTPRoute
202		grpcGroupKind    = v1.GroupName + "/" + kinds.GRPCRoute
203		serviceGroupKind = "core" + "/" + kinds.Service
204		// plmDefaultAccessKeyID is the fixed S3 access key ID configured by the SeaweedFS operator.
205		plmDefaultAccessKeyID = "adminKey"
206	)
207	
208	// attachPolicies attaches the graph's processed policies to the resources they target. It modifies the graph in place.
209	// extractExistingNGFGatewayAncestorsForPolicy extracts existing NGF gateway ancestors from policy status.
210	func extractExistingNGFGatewayAncestorsForPolicy(policy *Policy, ctlrName string) map[types.NamespacedName]struct{} {
211		existingNGFGatewayAncestors := make(map[types.NamespacedName]struct{})
212	
213		for _, ancestor := range policy.Source.GetPolicyStatus().Ancestors {
214			if string(ancestor.ControllerName) != ctlrName {
215				continue
216			}
217	
218			if ancestor.AncestorRef.Kind != nil && *ancestor.AncestorRef.Kind == v1.Kind(kinds.Gateway) &&
219				ancestor.AncestorRef.Namespace != nil {
220				gatewayNsName := types.NamespacedName{
221					Namespace: string(*ancestor.AncestorRef.Namespace),
222					Name:      string(ancestor.AncestorRef.Name),
223				}
224				existingNGFGatewayAncestors[gatewayNsName] = struct{}{}
225			}
226		}
227	
228		return existingNGFGatewayAncestors
229	}
230	
231	// collectOrderedGatewaysForService collects gateways for a service with existing gateway prioritization.
232	func collectOrderedGatewaysForService(
233		svc *ReferencedService,
234		gateways map[types.NamespacedName]*Gateway,
235		existingNGFGatewayAncestors map[types.NamespacedName]struct{},
236	) []types.NamespacedName {
237		existingGateways := make([]types.NamespacedName, 0, len(svc.GatewayNsNames))
238		newGateways := make([]types.NamespacedName, 0, len(svc.GatewayNsNames))
239	
240		for gwNsName := range svc.GatewayNsNames {
241			if _, exists := existingNGFGatewayAncestors[gwNsName]; exists {
242				existingGateways = append(existingGateways, gwNsName)
243			} else {
244				newGateways = append(newGateways, gwNsName)
245			}
246		}
247	
248		sortGatewaysByCreationTime(existingGateways, gateways)
249		sortGatewaysByCreationTime(newGateways, gateways)
250	
251		return append(existingGateways, newGateways...)
252	}
253	
254	func (g *Graph) attachPolicies(validator validation.PolicyValidator, ctlrName string, logger logr.Logger) {
255		if len(g.Gateways) == 0 {
256			return
257		}
258	
259		for _, policy := range g.NGFPolicies {
260			for _, ref := range policy.TargetRefs {
261				switch ref.Kind {
262				case kinds.Gateway:
263					attachPolicyToGateway(policy, ref, g.Gateways, g.Routes, ctlrName, logger, validator)
264				case kinds.HTTPRoute, kinds.GRPCRoute:
265					route, exists := g.Routes[routeKeyForKind(ref.Kind, ref.Nsname)]
266					if !exists {
267						continue
268					}
269	
270					attachPolicyToRoute(policy, route, validator, ctlrName, logger)
271				case kinds.Service:
272					svc, exists := g.ReferencedServices[ref.Nsname]
273					if !exists {
274						continue
275					}
276	
277					attachPolicyToService(policy, svc, g.Gateways, ctlrName, logger)
278				}
279			}
280		}
281	}
282	
283	func attachPolicyToService(
284		policy *Policy,
285		svc *ReferencedService,
286		gws map[types.NamespacedName]*Gateway,
287		ctlrName string,
288		logger logr.Logger,
289	) {
290		var attachedToAnyGateway bool
291	
292		// Extract existing NGF gateway ancestors from policy status
293		existingNGFGatewayAncestors := extractExistingNGFGatewayAncestorsForPolicy(policy, ctlrName)
294	
295		// Collect and order gateways with existing gateway prioritization
296		orderedGateways := collectOrderedGatewaysForService(svc, gws, existingNGFGatewayAncestors)
297	
298		for _, gwNsName := range orderedGateways {
299			gw := gws[gwNsName]
300	
301			if gw == nil || gw.Source == nil {
302				continue
303			}
304	
305			ancestorRef := createParentReference(v1.GroupName, kinds.Gateway, client.ObjectKeyFromObject(gw.Source))
306			ancestor := PolicyAncestor{
307				Ancestor: ancestorRef,
308			}
309	
310			if _, ok := policy.InvalidForGateways[gwNsName]; ok {
311				continue
312			}
313	
314			if ancestorsContainsAncestorRef(policy.Ancestors, ancestor.Ancestor) {
315				// Ancestor already exists, but we should still consider this gateway as attached
316				attachedToAnyGateway = true
317				continue
318			}
319	
320			// Check if this is an existing gateway from policy status
321			_, isExistingGateway := existingNGFGatewayAncestors[gwNsName]
322	
323			if isExistingGateway {
324				// Existing gateway from policy status - mark as attached but don't add to ancestors
325				attachedToAnyGateway = true
326				continue
327			}
328	
329			if ngfPolicyAncestorsFull(policy, ctlrName) {
330				policyName := getPolicyName(policy.Source)
331				policyKind := getPolicyKind(policy.Source)
332	
333				gw.Conditions = addPolicyAncestorLimitCondition(gw.Conditions, policyName, policyKind)
334				logAncestorLimitReached(logger, policyName, policyKind, gwNsName.String())
335	
336				// Mark this gateway as invalid for the policy due to ancestor limits
337				policy.InvalidForGateways[gwNsName] = struct{}{}
338				continue
339			}
340	
341			if !gw.Valid {
342				policy.InvalidForGateways[gwNsName] = struct{}{}
343				ancestor.Conditions = []conditions.Condition{conditions.NewPolicyTargetNotFound("The Parent Gateway is invalid")}
344				policy.Ancestors = append(policy.Ancestors, ancestor)
345				continue
346			}
347	
348			// Gateway is valid, add ancestor and mark as attached
349			policy.Ancestors = append(policy.Ancestors, ancestor)
350			attachedToAnyGateway = true
351		}
352	
353		// Attach policy to service if effective for at least one gateway
354		if attachedToAnyGateway {
355			svc.Policies = append(svc.Policies, policy)
356		}
357	}
358	
359	func attachPolicyToRoute(
360		policy *Policy,
361		route *L7Route,
362		validator validation.PolicyValidator,
363		ctlrName string,
364		logger logr.Logger,
365	) {
366		var effectiveGateways []types.NamespacedName
367	
368		kind := v1.Kind(kinds.HTTPRoute)
369		if route.RouteType == RouteTypeGRPC {
370			kind = kinds.GRPCRoute
371		}
372	
373		routeNsName := types.NamespacedName{Namespace: route.Source.GetNamespace(), Name: route.Source.GetName()}
374		ancestorRef := createParentReference(v1.GroupName, kind, routeNsName)
375	
376		// Check ancestor limit
377		isFull := ngfPolicyAncestorsFull(policy, ctlrName)
378		if isFull {
379			policyName := getPolicyName(policy.Source)
380			policyKind := getPolicyKind(policy.Source)
381			routeName := getAncestorName(ancestorRef)
382	
383			route.Conditions = addPolicyAncestorLimitCondition(route.Conditions, policyName, policyKind)
384			logAncestorLimitReached(logger, policyName, policyKind, routeName)
385	
386			return
387		}
388	
389		ancestor := PolicyAncestor{
390			Ancestor: ancestorRef,
391		}
392	
393		if !route.Valid || !route.Attachable || len(route.ParentRefs) == 0 {
394			ancestor.Conditions = []conditions.Condition{conditions.NewPolicyTargetNotFound("The TargetRef is invalid")}
395			policy.Ancestors = append(policy.Ancestors, ancestor)
396			return
397		}
398	
399		for _, parentRef := range route.ParentRefs {
400			if parentRef.EffectiveNginxProxy != nil {
401				globalSettings := &policies.GlobalSettings{
402					TelemetryEnabled: telemetryEnabledForNginxProxy(parentRef.EffectiveNginxProxy),
403					WAFEnabled:       WAFEnabledForNginxProxy(parentRef.EffectiveNginxProxy),
404				}
405	
406				if conds := validator.ValidateGlobalSettings(policy.Source, globalSettings); len(conds) > 0 {
407					policy.InvalidForGateways[parentRef.GatewayNsName] = struct{}{}
408					ancestor.Conditions = append(ancestor.Conditions, conds...)
409				} else {
410					// Policy is effective for this gateway (not adding to InvalidForGateways)
411					effectiveGateways = append(effectiveGateways, parentRef.GatewayNsName)
412				}
413			}
414		}
415	
416		policy.Ancestors = append(policy.Ancestors, ancestor)
417	
418		// Only attach policy to route if it's effective for at least one gateway
419		if len(effectiveGateways) > 0 || len(policy.InvalidForGateways) < len(route.ParentRefs) {
420			route.Policies = append(route.Policies, policy)
421		}
422	}
423	
424	func attachPolicyToGateway(
425		policy *Policy,
426		ref PolicyTargetRef,
427		gateways map[types.NamespacedName]*Gateway,
428		routes map[RouteKey]*L7Route,
429		ctlrName string,
430		logger logr.Logger,
431		validator validation.PolicyValidator,
432	) {
433		ancestorRef := createParentReference(v1.GroupName, kinds.Gateway, ref.Nsname)
434		gw, exists := gateways[ref.Nsname]
435	
436		if _, ok := policy.InvalidForGateways[ref.Nsname]; ok {
437			return
438		}
439	
440		if ancestorsContainsAncestorRef(policy.Ancestors, ancestorRef) {
441			// Ancestor already exists, but still attach policy to gateway if it's valid
442			if exists && gw != nil && gw.Valid && gw.Source != nil {
443				gw.Policies = append(gw.Policies, policy)
444				propagateSnippetsPolicyToRoutes(policy, gw, routes)
445			}
446			return
447		}
448		isFull := ngfPolicyAncestorsFull(policy, ctlrName)
449		if isFull {
450			ancestorName := getAncestorName(ancestorRef)
451			policyName := getPolicyName(policy.Source)
452			policyKind := getPolicyKind(policy.Source)
453	
454			if exists {
455				gw.Conditions = addPolicyAncestorLimitCondition(gw.Conditions, policyName, policyKind)
456			} else {
457				// Situation where gateway target is not found and the ancestors slice is full so I cannot add the condition.
458				// Log in the controller log.
459				logger.Info("Gateway target not found and ancestors slice is full.", "policy", policyName, "ancestor", ancestorName)
460			}
461			logAncestorLimitReached(logger, policyName, policyKind, ancestorName)
462	
463			policy.InvalidForGateways[ref.Nsname] = struct{}{}
464			return
465		}
466	
467		ancestor := PolicyAncestor{
468			Ancestor: ancestorRef,
469		}
470	
471		if !exists || (gw != nil && gw.Source == nil) {
472			policy.InvalidForGateways[ref.Nsname] = struct{}{}
473			ancestor.Conditions = []conditions.Condition{conditions.NewPolicyTargetNotFound("The TargetRef is not found")}
474			policy.Ancestors = append(policy.Ancestors, ancestor)
475			return
476		}
477	
478		if !gw.Valid {
479			policy.InvalidForGateways[ref.Nsname] = struct{}{}
480			ancestor.Conditions = []conditions.Condition{conditions.NewPolicyTargetNotFound("The TargetRef is invalid")}
481			policy.Ancestors = append(policy.Ancestors, ancestor)
482			return
483		}
484	
485		globalSettings := &policies.GlobalSettings{
486			TelemetryEnabled: telemetryEnabledForNginxProxy(gw.EffectiveNginxProxy),
487			WAFEnabled:       WAFEnabledForNginxProxy(gw.EffectiveNginxProxy),
488		}
489	
490		// Policy is effective for this gateway (not adding to InvalidForGateways)
491		if conds := validator.ValidateGlobalSettings(policy.Source, globalSettings); len(conds) > 0 {
492			ancestor.Conditions = conds
493			policy.Ancestors = append(policy.Ancestors, ancestor)
494			return
495		}
496	
497		policy.Ancestors = append(policy.Ancestors, ancestor)
498		gw.Policies = append(gw.Policies, policy)
499		propagateSnippetsPolicyToRoutes(policy, gw, routes)
500	}
501	
502	func propagateSnippetsPolicyToRoutes(
503		policy *Policy,
504		gw *Gateway,
505		routes map[RouteKey]*L7Route,
506	) {
507		// Only SnippetsPolicy supports propagation from Gateway to Routes
508		if getPolicyKind(policy.Source) != kinds.SnippetsPolicy {
509			return
510		}
511	
512		gwNsName := client.ObjectKeyFromObject(gw.Source)
513	
514		for _, route := range routes {
515			for _, parentRef := range route.ParentRefs {
516				// Check if the route is attached to this specific gateway, either directly
517				// or via a ListenerSet (GatewayNsName resolves to the parent Gateway for both).
518				if parentRef.GatewayNsName == gwNsName {
519					// Avoid duplicate attachment if logic runs multiple times (though graph build is single pass)
520					// or if policy targets both.
521					alreadyAttached := slices.Contains(route.Policies, policy)
522					if !alreadyAttached {
523						route.Policies = append(route.Policies, policy)
524					}
525				}
526			}
527		}
528	}

530	// PLMResolvedSecrets holds S3 credentials and TLS config resolved from CLI-configured Kubernetes Secrets.
531	// Populated once per BuildGraph call so PLM fetch functions can use current secret data.
532	type PLMResolvedSecrets struct {
533		// Credentials holds the S3 access key ID and secret access key for PLM storage.
534		// Nil if no credentials secret was configured or if the secret is not yet available.
535		Credentials *s3fetch.Credentials
536		// TLS holds TLS CA cert and optional client cert/key.
537		// Nil if no TLS secrets were configured or available.
538		TLS *s3fetch.TLSConfig
539		// ResolutionError captures any configured-secret resolution failures.
540		// When non-nil, PLM fetches must fail closed instead of falling back to anonymous or default TLS behavior.
541		ResolutionError error
542	}
543	
544	// WAFProcessingInput contains the input needed for WAF policy processing.
545	type WAFProcessingInput struct {
546		// Fetcher fetches bundle files from HTTP/HTTPS URLs.
547		Fetcher fetch.Fetcher
548		// PLMFetcher fetches bundle files from PLM's S3-compatible storage.
549		// Nil if PLM is not configured.
550		PLMFetcher *s3fetch.Fetcher
551		// Secrets contains the Secrets from the cluster, used to resolve bundle auth credentials.
552		Secrets map[types.NamespacedName]*corev1.Secret
553		// PreviousBundles contains the bundles successfully fetched in the previous processing cycle.
554		// Used to keep the last-known-good bundle active when a re-fetch fails.
555		PreviousBundles map[WAFBundleKey]*WAFBundleData
556		// APPolicies contains the APPolicy resources from the cluster (appprotect.f5.com/v1).
557		// Nil if PLM CRDs are not installed.
558		APPolicies map[types.NamespacedName]*unstructured.Unstructured
559		// APLogConfs contains the APLogConf resources from the cluster (appprotect.f5.com/v1).
560		// Nil if PLM CRDs are not installed.
561		APLogConfs map[types.NamespacedName]*unstructured.Unstructured
562		// RefGrantResolver resolves ReferenceGrant permissions for cross-namespace references.
563		RefGrantResolver *referenceGrantResolver
564		// PLMResolvedSecrets holds resolved S3 credentials and TLS config for PLM bundle fetching.
565		// Nil if PLM is not configured.
566		PLMResolvedSecrets *PLMResolvedSecrets
567	}
568	
569	// WAFProcessingOutput contains the output from WAF policy processing.
570	type WAFProcessingOutput struct {
571		// Bundles contains the fetched WAF bundles keyed by bundle key.
572		Bundles map[WAFBundleKey]*WAFBundleData
573		// ReferencedAPPolicies contains APPolicy resources referenced by WAFPolicy resources.
574		ReferencedAPPolicies map[types.NamespacedName]*unstructured.Unstructured
575		// ReferencedAPLogConfs contains APLogConf resources referenced by WAFPolicy resources.
576		ReferencedAPLogConfs map[types.NamespacedName]*unstructured.Unstructured
577		// ReferencedWAFSecrets contains the Secrets referenced by WAFPolicy (auth and TLS CA).
578		// These must be watched by the change tracker.
579		ReferencedWAFSecrets map[types.NamespacedName]*corev1.Secret
580	}
581	
582	func processPolicies(
583		ctx context.Context,
584		logger logr.Logger,
585		pols map[PolicyKey]policies.Policy,
586		validator validation.PolicyValidator,
587		routes map[RouteKey]*L7Route,
588		services map[types.NamespacedName]*ReferencedService,
589		gws map[types.NamespacedName]*Gateway,
590		wafInput *WAFProcessingInput,
591	) (map[PolicyKey]*Policy, *WAFProcessingOutput) {
592		if len(pols) == 0 || len(gws) == 0 {
593			return nil, nil
594		}
595	
596		processedPolicies := make(map[PolicyKey]*Policy)
597	
598		for key, policy := range pols {
599			var conds []conditions.Condition
600	
601			targetRefs := make([]PolicyTargetRef, 0, len(policy.GetTargetRefs()))
602			targetedRoutes := make(map[types.NamespacedName]*L7Route)
603	
604			for _, ref := range policy.GetTargetRefs() {
605				refNsName := types.NamespacedName{Name: string(ref.Name), Namespace: policy.GetNamespace()}
606	
607				switch refGroupKind(ref.Group, ref.Kind) {
608				case gatewayGroupKind:
609					if !gatewayExists(refNsName, gws) {
610						continue
611					}
612				case hrGroupKind, grpcGroupKind:
613					if route, exists := routes[routeKeyForKind(ref.Kind, refNsName)]; exists {
614						targetedRoutes[client.ObjectKeyFromObject(route.Source)] = route
615					} else {
616						continue
617					}
618				case serviceGroupKind:
619					if _, exists := services[refNsName]; !exists {
620						continue
621					}
622				default:
623					continue
624				}
625	
626				targetRefs = append(targetRefs,
627					PolicyTargetRef{
628						Kind:   ref.Kind,
629						Group:  ref.Group,
630						Nsname: refNsName,
631					})
632			}
633	
634			if len(targetRefs) == 0 {
635				continue
636			}
637	
638			overlapConds := checkTargetRoutesForOverlap(targetedRoutes, routes)
639			conds = append(conds, overlapConds...)
640	
641			conds = append(conds, validator.Validate(policy)...)
642	
643			processedPolicies[key] = &Policy{
644				Source:             policy,
645				Valid:              len(conds) == 0,
646				Conditions:         conds,
647				TargetRefs:         targetRefs,
648				Ancestors:          make([]PolicyAncestor, 0, len(targetRefs)),
649				InvalidForGateways: make(map[types.NamespacedName]struct{}),
650			}
651		}
652	
653		markConflictedPolicies(processedPolicies, validator)
654	
655		wafOutput := processWAFPolicies(ctx, logger, processedPolicies, wafInput)
656	
657		return processedPolicies, wafOutput
658	}
659	
660	func checkTargetRoutesForOverlap(
661		targetedRoutes map[types.NamespacedName]*L7Route,
662		graphRoutes map[RouteKey]*L7Route,
663	) []conditions.Condition {
664		var conds []conditions.Condition
665	
666		for _, targetedRoute := range targetedRoutes {
667			// We need to check if this route referenced in the policy has an overlapping
668			// namespace/gateway-name:hostname:port/path with any other route that isn't referenced by this policy.
669			// If so, deny the policy.
670			gatewayHostPortPaths := buildGatewayHostPortPaths(targetedRoute)
671	
672			for _, route := range graphRoutes {
673				if _, ok := targetedRoutes[client.ObjectKeyFromObject(route.Source)]; ok {
674					continue
675				}
676	
677				if cond := checkForRouteOverlap(route, gatewayHostPortPaths); cond != nil {
678					conds = append(conds, *cond)
679				}
680			}
681		}
682	
683		return conds
684	}
685	
686	// checkForRouteOverlap checks if a non-targeted route references the same
687	// namespace/gateway-name:hostname:port/path combination as a targeted route in the policy.
688	// It only reads from the gatewayHostPortPaths map — it does not mutate it.
689	// This prevents two unrelated non-targeted routes from triggering a false-positive conflict.
690	func checkForRouteOverlap(route *L7Route, gatewayHostPortPaths map[string]string) *conditions.Condition {
691		currentRouteName := fmt.Sprintf("%s/%s", route.Source.GetNamespace(), route.Source.GetName())
692	
693		for _, parentRef := range route.ParentRefs {
694			if parentRef.Attachment != nil {
695				port := parentRef.Attachment.ListenerPort
696				// FIXME(sarthyparty): https://github.com/nginx/nginx-gateway-fabric/issues/3811
697				// Need to merge listener hostnames with route hostnames so wildcards are handled correctly
698				// Also the AcceptedHostnames is a map of slices of strings, so we need to flatten it
699				for _, hostname := range parentRef.Attachment.AcceptedHostnames {
700					for _, rule := range route.Spec.Rules {
701						for _, match := range rule.Matches {
702							if match.Path != nil && match.Path.Value != nil {
703								// Use GatewayNsName to ensure overlap detection works across routes
704								// attached directly to a Gateway and those attached via ListenerSet.
705								key := fmt.Sprintf(
706									"%s:%s:%d%s",
707									parentRef.GatewayNsName.String(),
708									hostname,
709									port,
710									*match.Path.Value,
711								)
712								if val, ok := gatewayHostPortPaths[key]; ok && val != currentRouteName {
713									msg := fmt.Sprintf(
714										"Policy cannot be applied to target %q since another "+
715											"Route %q shares a namespace/gateway-name:hostname:port/path combination with this target",
716										val, currentRouteName,
717									)
718									cond := conditions.NewPolicyNotAcceptedTargetConflict(msg)
719	
720									return &cond
721								}
722							}
723						}
724					}
725				}
726			}
727		}
728	
729		return nil
730	}
731	
732	// buildGatewayHostPortPaths builds a map of namespace/gateway-name:hostname:port/path keys
733	// for a route that is targeted by the policy.
734	func buildGatewayHostPortPaths(route *L7Route) map[string]string {
735		gatewayHostPortPaths := make(map[string]string)
736		routeName := fmt.Sprintf("%s/%s", route.Source.GetNamespace(), route.Source.GetName())
737	
738		for _, parentRef := range route.ParentRefs {
739			if parentRef.Attachment != nil {
740				port := parentRef.Attachment.ListenerPort
741				for _, hostname := range parentRef.Attachment.AcceptedHostnames {
742					for _, rule := range route.Spec.Rules {
743						for _, match := range rule.Matches {
744							if match.Path != nil && match.Path.Value != nil {
745								key := fmt.Sprintf(
746									"%s:%s:%d%s",
747									parentRef.GatewayNsName.String(),
748									hostname,
749									port,
750									*match.Path.Value,
751								)
752								gatewayHostPortPaths[key] = routeName
753							}
754						}
755					}
756				}
757			}
758		}
759	
760		return gatewayHostPortPaths
761	}
762	
763	// markConflictedPolicies marks policies that conflict with a policy of greater precedence as invalid.
764	// Policies are sorted by timestamp and then alphabetically.
765	func markConflictedPolicies(pols map[PolicyKey]*Policy, validator validation.PolicyValidator) {
766		// Policies can only conflict if they are the same policy type (gvk) and they target the same resource(s).
767		type key struct {
768			policyGVK schema.GroupVersionKind
769			PolicyTargetRef
770		}
771	
772		possibles := make(map[key][]*Policy)
773	
774		for policyKey, policy := range pols {
775			// If a policy is invalid, it cannot conflict with another policy.
776			if policy.Valid {
777				for _, ref := range policy.TargetRefs {
778					ak := key{
779						PolicyTargetRef: ref,
780						policyGVK:       policyKey.GVK,
781					}
782					if possibles[ak] == nil {
783						possibles[ak] = make([]*Policy, 0)
784					}
785					possibles[ak] = append(possibles[ak], policy)
786				}
787			}
788		}
789	
790		for _, policyList := range possibles {
791			if len(policyList) == 1 {
792				// if the policyList only has one entry, then we don't need to check for conflicts.
793				continue
794			}
795	
796			// First, we sort the policyList according to the rules in the spec.
797			// This will put them in priority-order.
798			sort.Slice(
799				policyList, func(i, j int) bool {
800					return ngfsort.LessClientObject(policyList[i].Source, policyList[j].Source)
801				},
802			)
803	
804			// Second, we range over the policyList, starting with the highest priority policy.
805			for i := range policyList {
806				if !policyList[i].Valid {
807					// Ignore policy that has already been marked as invalid.
808					continue
809				}
810	
811				// Next, we compare the ith policy (policyList[i]) to the rest of the policies in the list.
812				// The ith policy takes precedence over polices that follow it, so if there is a conflict between
813				// it and a subsequent policy, the ith policy wins, and we mark the subsequent policy as invalid.
814				// Example: policyList = [A, B, C] where B conflicts with A.
815				// i=A, j=B => conflict, B's marked as invalid.
816				// i=A, j=C => no conflict.
817				// i=B, j=C => B's already invalid, so we hit the continue.
818				// i=C => j loop terminates.
819				// Results: A, and C are valid. B is invalid.
820				for j := i + 1; j < len(policyList); j++ {
821					if !policyList[j].Valid {
822						// Ignore policy that has already been marked as invalid.
823						continue
824					}
825	
826					if validator.Conflicts(policyList[i].Source, policyList[j].Source) {
827						conflicted := policyList[j]
828						conflicted.Valid = false
829						conflicted.Conditions = append(conflicted.Conditions, conditions.NewPolicyConflicted(
830							fmt.Sprintf(
831								"Conflicts with another %s",
832								conflicted.Source.GetObjectKind().GroupVersionKind().Kind,
833							),
834						))
835					}
836				}
837			}
838		}
839	}
840	
841	// refGroupKind formats the group and kind as a string.
842	func refGroupKind(group v1.Group, kind v1.Kind) string {
843		if group == "" {
844			return fmt.Sprintf("core/%s", kind)
845		}
846	
847		return fmt.Sprintf("%s/%s", group, kind)
848	}
849	
850	// addPolicyAffectedStatusToTargetRefs adds the policyAffected status to the target references
851	// of ClientSettingsPolicies and ObservabilityPolicies.
852	func addPolicyAffectedStatusToTargetRefs(
853		processedPolicies map[PolicyKey]*Policy,
854		routes map[RouteKey]*L7Route,
855		gws map[types.NamespacedName]*Gateway,
856	) {
857		for policyKey, policy := range processedPolicies {
858			for _, ref := range policy.TargetRefs {
859				switch ref.Kind {
860				case kinds.Gateway:
861					if !gatewayExists(ref.Nsname, gws) {
862						continue
863					}
864					gw := gws[ref.Nsname]
865					if gw == nil {
866						continue
867					}
868	
869					// set the policy status on the Gateway.
870					policyKind := policyKey.GVK.Kind
871					addStatusToTargetRefs(policyKind, &gw.Conditions)
872				case kinds.HTTPRoute, kinds.GRPCRoute:
873					routeKey := routeKeyForKind(ref.Kind, ref.Nsname)
874					l7route, exists := routes[routeKey]
875					if !exists {
876						continue
877					}
878	
879					// set the policy status on L7 routes.
880					policyKind := policyKey.GVK.Kind
881					addStatusToTargetRefs(policyKind, &l7route.Conditions)
882				default:
883					continue
884				}
885			}
886		}
887	}
888	
889	func addStatusToTargetRefs(policyKind string, conditionsList *[]conditions.Condition) {
890		if conditionsList == nil {
891			return
892		}
893		switch policyKind {
894		case kinds.ObservabilityPolicy:
895			if conditions.HasMatchingCondition(*conditionsList, conditions.NewObservabilityPolicyAffected()) {
896				return
897			}
898			*conditionsList = append(*conditionsList, conditions.NewObservabilityPolicyAffected())
899		case kinds.ClientSettingsPolicy:
900			if conditions.HasMatchingCondition(*conditionsList, conditions.NewClientSettingsPolicyAffected()) {
901				return
902			}
903			*conditionsList = append(*conditionsList, conditions.NewClientSettingsPolicyAffected())
904		case kinds.SnippetsPolicy:
905			if conditions.HasMatchingCondition(*conditionsList, conditions.NewSnippetsPolicyAffected()) {
906				return
907			}
908			*conditionsList = append(*conditionsList, conditions.NewSnippetsPolicyAffected())
909		case kinds.ProxySettingsPolicy:
910			if conditions.HasMatchingCondition(*conditionsList, conditions.NewProxySettingsPolicyAffected()) {
911				return
912			}
913			*conditionsList = append(*conditionsList, conditions.NewProxySettingsPolicyAffected())
914		case kinds.RateLimitPolicy:
915			if conditions.HasMatchingCondition(*conditionsList, conditions.NewRateLimitPolicyAffected()) {
916				return
917			}
918			*conditionsList = append(*conditionsList, conditions.NewRateLimitPolicyAffected())
919		case kinds.WAFPolicy:
920			if conditions.HasMatchingCondition(*conditionsList, conditions.NewWAFPolicyAffected()) {
921				return
922			}
923			*conditionsList = append(*conditionsList, conditions.NewWAFPolicyAffected())
924		}
925	}

927	// processWAFPolicies processes WAFPolicy resources and fetches their bundles.
928	func processWAFPolicies(
929		ctx context.Context,
930		logger logr.Logger,
931		processedPolicies map[PolicyKey]*Policy,
932		wafInput *WAFProcessingInput,
933	) *WAFProcessingOutput {
934		if wafInput == nil {
935			return nil
936		}
937	
938		output := &WAFProcessingOutput{
939			Bundles:              make(map[WAFBundleKey]*WAFBundleData),
940			ReferencedAPPolicies: make(map[types.NamespacedName]*unstructured.Unstructured),
941			ReferencedAPLogConfs: make(map[types.NamespacedName]*unstructured.Unstructured),
942			ReferencedWAFSecrets: make(map[types.NamespacedName]*corev1.Secret),
943		}
944	
945		for key, policy := range processedPolicies {
946			if key.GVK.Kind != kinds.WAFPolicy {
947				continue
948			}
949	
950			if !policy.Valid {
951				continue
952			}
953	
954			wgbPolicy, ok := policy.Source.(*ngfAPIv1alpha1.WAFPolicy)
955			if !ok {
956				continue
957			}
958	
959			// Initialize the WAFBundles map on the policy to store fetched bundles.
960			// This allows each gateway to receive only the bundles for policies that target it.
961			policy.WAFState = &PolicyWAFState{
962				Bundles: make(map[WAFBundleKey]*WAFBundleData),
963			}
964	
965			if wgbPolicy.Spec.Type == ngfAPIv1alpha1.PolicySourceTypePLM {
966				fetchPLMPolicyBundle(ctx, logger, wgbPolicy, policy, wafInput, output)
967				fetchPLMSecurityLogBundles(ctx, logger, wgbPolicy, policy, wafInput, output)
968			} else {
969				fetchPolicyBundle(ctx, logger, wgbPolicy, policy, wafInput, output)
970				fetchSecurityLogBundles(ctx, logger, wgbPolicy, policy, wafInput, output)
971			}
972		}
973	
974		return output
975	}
976	
977	// BuildPolicyFetchRequest constructs a fetch.Request from a PolicySource, resolved auth, and TLS CA data.
978	//
979	//nolint:gocyclo // complexity is inherent to handling HTTP/NIM/N1C source types with different field structures
980	func BuildPolicyFetchRequest(
981		policySource *ngfAPIv1alpha1.PolicySource,
982		policyType ngfAPIv1alpha1.PolicySourceType,
983		auth *fetch.BundleAuth,
984		tlsCA []byte,
985	) fetch.Request {
986		if policySource == nil {
987			return fetch.Request{}
988		}
989		req := fetch.Request{
990			Auth:               auth,
991			TLSCAData:          tlsCA,
992			InsecureSkipVerify: policySource.InsecureSkipVerify,
993			VerifyChecksum:     policySource.Validation != nil && policySource.Validation.VerifyChecksum,
994			ExpectedChecksum:   expectedChecksum(policySource.Validation),
995			Timeout:            policySource.Timeout,
996			RetryAttempts:      retryAttempts(policySource.RetryAttempts),
997		}
998	
999		switch policyType {
1000		case ngfAPIv1alpha1.PolicySourceTypeHTTP:
1001			if policySource.HTTPSource != nil {
1002				req.URL = policySource.HTTPSource.URL
1003			}
1004		case ngfAPIv1alpha1.PolicySourceTypeNIM:
1005			if policySource.NIMSource != nil {
1006				req.URL = policySource.NIMSource.URL
1007				if policySource.NIMSource.PolicyUID != nil {
1008					req.NIM.PolicyUID = *policySource.NIMSource.PolicyUID
1009				} else if policySource.NIMSource.PolicyName != nil {
1010					req.PolicyName = *policySource.NIMSource.PolicyName
1011				}
1012			}
1013		case ngfAPIv1alpha1.PolicySourceTypeN1C:
1014			if policySource.N1CSource != nil {
1015				req.URL = policySource.N1CSource.URL
1016				req.N1C.Namespace = policySource.N1CSource.Namespace
1017				if policySource.N1CSource.PolicyObjectID != nil {
1018					req.N1C.PolicyObjectID = *policySource.N1CSource.PolicyObjectID
1019				}
1020				if policySource.N1CSource.PolicyName != nil {
1021					req.PolicyName = *policySource.N1CSource.PolicyName
1022				}
1023				if policySource.N1CSource.PolicyVersionID != nil {
1024					req.N1C.PolicyVersionID = *policySource.N1CSource.PolicyVersionID
1025				}
1026				// N1C uses the APIToken auth scheme rather than Bearer.
1027				// Move the token value from BearerToken to APIToken.
1028				if auth != nil && auth.BearerToken != "" {
1029					req.Auth = &fetch.BundleAuth{APIToken: auth.BearerToken}
1030				}
1031			}
1032		}
1033	
1034		return req
1035	}
1036	
1037	// BuildLogFetchRequest constructs a fetch.Request from a LogSource, resolved auth, and TLS CA data.
1038	func BuildLogFetchRequest(
1039		logSource *ngfAPIv1alpha1.LogSource,
1040		auth *fetch.BundleAuth,
1041		tlsCA []byte,
1042	) fetch.Request {
1043		if logSource == nil {
1044			return fetch.Request{}
1045		}
1046		req := fetch.Request{
1047			Auth:               auth,
1048			TLSCAData:          tlsCA,
1049			InsecureSkipVerify: logSource.InsecureSkipVerify,
1050			VerifyChecksum:     logSource.Validation != nil && logSource.Validation.VerifyChecksum,
1051			ExpectedChecksum:   expectedChecksum(logSource.Validation),
1052			Timeout:            logSource.Timeout,
1053			RetryAttempts:      retryAttempts(logSource.RetryAttempts),
1054		}
1055	
1056		switch {
1057		case logSource.NIMSource != nil:
1058			if logSource.NIMSource.URL != "" {
1059				req.URL = logSource.NIMSource.URL
1060			}
1061			if logSource.NIMSource.ProfileName != "" {
1062				req.LogProfileName = logSource.NIMSource.ProfileName
1063			}
1064		case logSource.N1CSource != nil:
1065			req.URL = logSource.N1CSource.URL
1066			req.N1C.Namespace = logSource.N1CSource.Namespace
1067			if logSource.N1CSource.ProfileObjectID != nil {
1068				req.N1C.LogProfileObjectID = *logSource.N1CSource.ProfileObjectID
1069			}
1070			if logSource.N1CSource.ProfileName != nil {
1071				req.LogProfileName = *logSource.N1CSource.ProfileName
1072			}
1073			// N1C uses the APIToken auth scheme rather than Bearer.
1074			// Move the token value from BearerToken to APIToken.
1075			if auth != nil && auth.BearerToken != "" {
1076				req.Auth = &fetch.BundleAuth{APIToken: auth.BearerToken}
1077			}
1078		case logSource.HTTPSource != nil:
1079			if logSource.HTTPSource.URL != "" {
1080				req.URL = logSource.HTTPSource.URL
1081			}
1082		}
1083	
1084		return req
1085	}
1086	
1087	// retryAttempts dereferences a retry attempts pointer, returning the default of 3 when nil.
1088	func retryAttempts(attempts *int32) int32 {
1089		if attempts == nil {
1090			return 3
1091		}
1092		return *attempts
1093	}
1094	
1095	// fetchPolicyBundle fetches the policy bundle for a WAFPolicy.
1096	func fetchPolicyBundle(
1097		ctx context.Context,
1098		logger logr.Logger,
1099		wafPolicy *ngfAPIv1alpha1.WAFPolicy,
1100		policy *Policy,
1101		wafInput *WAFProcessingInput,
1102		output *WAFProcessingOutput,
1103	) {
1104		policySource := wafPolicy.Spec.PolicySource
1105		if policySource == nil {
1106			return
1107		}
1108	
1109		var auth *fetch.BundleAuth
1110		if policySource.Auth != nil {
1111			var cond *conditions.Condition
1112			auth, cond = resolveBundleAuth(policySource.Auth, wafPolicy.Namespace, wafInput, output)
1113			if cond != nil {
1114				policy.Conditions = append(policy.Conditions, *cond)
1115				policy.Valid = false
1116				return
1117			}
1118		}
1119	
1120		var tlsCA []byte
1121		if policySource.TLSSecretRef != nil {
1122			var cond *conditions.Condition
1123			tlsCA, cond = resolveTLSCA(policySource.TLSSecretRef, wafPolicy.Namespace, wafInput, output)
1124			if cond != nil {
1125				policy.Conditions = append(policy.Conditions, *cond)
1126				policy.Valid = false
1127				return
1128			}
1129		}
1130	
1131		// Store resolved auth/TLS for use by the WAF polling manager.
1132		policy.WAFState.ResolvedAuth = auth
1133		policy.WAFState.ResolvedTLSCA = tlsCA
1134	
1135		bundleKey := PolicyBundleKey(types.NamespacedName{Namespace: wafPolicy.Namespace, Name: wafPolicy.Name})
1136	
1137		req := BuildPolicyFetchRequest(policySource, wafPolicy.Spec.Type, auth, tlsCA)
1138	
1139		result, err := wafInput.Fetcher.FetchPolicyBundle(ctx, req)
1140		if err != nil {
1141			logger.Error(err, "Failed to fetch WAF policy bundle", "resource", wafPolicy.Name)
1142			if prev, ok := wafInput.PreviousBundles[bundleKey]; ok {
1143				cond := conditions.NewPolicyProgrammedStaleBundleWarning("policy bundle", err.Error())
1144				policy.Conditions = append(policy.Conditions, cond)
1145				output.Bundles[bundleKey] = prev
1146				policy.WAFState.Bundles[bundleKey] = prev
1147				return
1148			}
1149			cond := conditions.NewPolicyNotProgrammedBundlePending(err.Error())
1150			policy.Conditions = append(policy.Conditions, cond)
1151			policy.WAFState.BundlePending = true
1152			return
1153		}
1154	
1155		bundleData := &WAFBundleData{Data: result.Data, Checksum: result.Checksum}
1156		output.Bundles[bundleKey] = bundleData
1157		policy.WAFState.Bundles[bundleKey] = bundleData
1158	}
1159	
1160	// fetchSecurityLogBundles fetches log profile bundles for each SecurityLog entry.
1161	func fetchSecurityLogBundles(
1162		ctx context.Context,
1163		logger logr.Logger,
1164		wafPolicy *ngfAPIv1alpha1.WAFPolicy,
1165		policy *Policy,
1166		wafInput *WAFProcessingInput,
1167		output *WAFProcessingOutput,
1168	) {
1169		for _, secLog := range wafPolicy.Spec.SecurityLogs {
1170			if secLog.LogSource == nil {
1171				continue
1172			}
1173	
1174			if secLog.LogSource.HTTPSource == nil && secLog.LogSource.NIMSource == nil && secLog.LogSource.N1CSource == nil {
1175				// DefaultProfile is used; no bundle to fetch.
1176				continue
1177			}
1178	
1179			var auth *fetch.BundleAuth
1180			if secLog.LogSource.Auth != nil {
1181				var cond *conditions.Condition
1182				auth, cond = resolveBundleAuth(secLog.LogSource.Auth, wafPolicy.Namespace, wafInput, output)
1183				if cond != nil {
1184					policy.Conditions = append(policy.Conditions, *cond)
1185					policy.Valid = false
1186					continue
1187				}
1188			}
1189	
1190			var tlsCA []byte
1191			if secLog.LogSource.TLSSecretRef != nil {
1192				var cond *conditions.Condition
1193				tlsCA, cond = resolveTLSCA(secLog.LogSource.TLSSecretRef, wafPolicy.Namespace, wafInput, output)
1194				if cond != nil {
1195					policy.Conditions = append(policy.Conditions, *cond)
1196					policy.Valid = false
1197					continue
1198				}
1199			}
1200	
1201			bundleKey := LogBundleKey(
1202				types.NamespacedName{Namespace: wafPolicy.Namespace, Name: wafPolicy.Name},
1203				secLog.LogSource,
1204			)
1205	
1206			// Multiple SecurityLog entries may reference the same URL and therefore produce the same
1207			// bundleKey. Once the bundle has been fetched, skip subsequent entries with the same key
1208			// to avoid redundant network calls and to prevent a failed fetch (e.g. due to different
1209			// auth settings on the duplicate entry) from invalidating the policy.
1210			if _, alreadyFetched := output.Bundles[bundleKey]; alreadyFetched {
1211				continue
1212			}
1213	
1214			req := BuildLogFetchRequest(secLog.LogSource, auth, tlsCA)
1215	
1216			result, err := wafInput.Fetcher.FetchLogProfileBundle(ctx, req)
1217			if err != nil {
1218				logger.Error(
1219					err,
1220					"Failed to fetch WAF security log bundle",
1221					"resource",
1222					wafPolicy.Name,
1223				)
1224				if prev, ok := wafInput.PreviousBundles[bundleKey]; ok {
1225					cond := conditions.NewPolicyProgrammedStaleBundleWarning(LogBundleDescription(secLog.LogSource), err.Error())
1226					policy.Conditions = append(policy.Conditions, cond)
1227					output.Bundles[bundleKey] = prev
1228					policy.WAFState.Bundles[bundleKey] = prev
1229					continue
1230				}
1231				cond := conditions.NewPolicyNotProgrammedBundlePending(err.Error())
1232				policy.Conditions = append(policy.Conditions, cond)
1233				policy.WAFState.BundlePending = true
1234				continue
1235			}
1236	
1237			bundleData := &WAFBundleData{Data: result.Data, Checksum: result.Checksum}
1238			output.Bundles[bundleKey] = bundleData
1239			policy.WAFState.Bundles[bundleKey] = bundleData
1240		}
1241	}
1242	
1243	// expectedChecksum returns the ExpectedChecksum value from a BundleValidation, or empty string if nil.
1244	func expectedChecksum(v *ngfAPIv1alpha1.BundleValidation) string {
1245		if v == nil || v.ExpectedChecksum == nil {
1246			return ""
1247		}
1248		return *v.ExpectedChecksum
1249	}
1250	
1251	// resolveBundleAuth resolves a BundleAuth reference into fetch.BundleAuth credentials.
1252	// It looks up the referenced Secret from wafInput.Secrets and adds it to output.ReferencedWAFSecrets.
1253	// bundleAuth must not be nil.
1254	// Returns a non-nil *conditions.Condition on failure so callers can append it directly;
1255	// uses NotFound for a missing Secret and Invalid for wrong/empty credential keys.
1256	func resolveBundleAuth(
1257		bundleAuth *ngfAPIv1alpha1.BundleAuth,
1258		policyNamespace string,
1259		wafInput *WAFProcessingInput,
1260		output *WAFProcessingOutput,
1261	) (*fetch.BundleAuth, *conditions.Condition) {
1262		secretNsName := types.NamespacedName{
1263			Namespace: policyNamespace,
1264			Name:      bundleAuth.SecretRef.Name,
1265		}
1266	
1267		secret, exists := wafInput.Secrets[secretNsName]
1268		if !exists {
1269			cond := conditions.NewPolicyRefsNotResolved(
1270				fmt.Sprintf("auth secret %q not found", secretNsName),
1271			)
1272			return nil, &cond
1273		}
1274	
1275		output.ReferencedWAFSecrets[secretNsName] = secret
1276	
1277		auth := &fetch.BundleAuth{}
1278		if token, ok := secret.Data[secrets.BundleTokenKey]; ok {
1279			auth.BearerToken = strings.TrimSpace(string(token))
1280			if auth.BearerToken == "" {
1281				cond := conditions.NewPolicyRefsNotResolved(
1282					fmt.Sprintf("auth secret %q has empty %q key", secretNsName, secrets.BundleTokenKey),
1283				)
1284				return nil, &cond
1285			}
1286		} else {
1287			auth.Username = strings.TrimSpace(string(secret.Data[secrets.BundleUsernameKey]))
1288			auth.Password = strings.TrimSpace(string(secret.Data[secrets.BundlePasswordKey]))
1289			if auth.Username == "" || auth.Password == "" {
1290				cond := conditions.NewPolicyRefsNotResolved(fmt.Sprintf(
1291					"auth secret %q must contain either %q or both %q and %q",
1292					secretNsName, secrets.BundleTokenKey, secrets.BundleUsernameKey, secrets.BundlePasswordKey,
1293				))
1294				return nil, &cond
1295			}
1296		}
1297	
1298		return auth, nil
1299	}
1300	
1301	// resolveTLSCA resolves a TLS CA secret reference into a PEM-encoded CA certificate byte slice.
1302	// It looks up the referenced Secret from wafInput.Secrets and adds it to output.ReferencedWAFSecrets.
1303	// tlsSecret must not be nil.
1304	// Returns a non-nil *conditions.Condition on failure so callers can append it directly;
1305	// uses NotFound for a missing Secret and Invalid for a missing ca.crt key.
1306	func resolveTLSCA(
1307		tlsSecret *ngfAPIv1alpha1.LocalObjectReference,
1308		policyNamespace string,
1309		wafInput *WAFProcessingInput,
1310		output *WAFProcessingOutput,
1311	) ([]byte, *conditions.Condition) {
1312		secretNsName := types.NamespacedName{
1313			Namespace: policyNamespace,
1314			Name:      tlsSecret.Name,
1315		}
1316	
1317		secret, exists := wafInput.Secrets[secretNsName]
1318		if !exists {
1319			cond := conditions.NewPolicyRefsNotResolved(
1320				fmt.Sprintf("TLS CA secret %q not found", secretNsName),
1321			)
1322			return nil, &cond
1323		}
1324	
1325		caData, ok := secret.Data[secrets.CAKey]
1326		if !ok {
1327			cond := conditions.NewPolicyRefsNotResolved(
1328				fmt.Sprintf("TLS CA secret %q missing %q key", secretNsName, secrets.CAKey),
1329			)
1330			return nil, &cond
1331		}
1332	
1333		if len(bytes.TrimSpace(caData)) == 0 {
1334			cond := conditions.NewPolicyRefsNotResolved(
1335				fmt.Sprintf("TLS CA secret %q has empty %q key", secretNsName, secrets.CAKey),
1336			)
1337			return nil, &cond
1338		}
1339	
1340		output.ReferencedWAFSecrets[secretNsName] = secret
1341	
1342		return caData, nil
1343	}

1345	// PLMPolicyBundleKey returns the WAFBundleKey for a PLM WAFPolicy's main policy bundle.

1350	// PLMLogBundleKey returns the WAFBundleKey for a PLM WAFPolicy's log profile bundle.
1351	func PLMLogBundleKey(
1352		policyNsName types.NamespacedName,
1353		ref *ngfAPIv1alpha1.APLogConfReference,
1354	) WAFBundleKey {

1356		if ref.Namespace != nil {
1357			ns = *ref.Namespace
1358		}
1359		return WAFBundleKey(fmt.Sprintf(
1360			"%s_%s_log_%s_%s",
1361			policyNsName.Namespace,
1362			policyNsName.Name,
1363			ns,
1364			ref.Name,
1365		))
1366	}
1367	
1368	// fetchPLMPolicyBundle fetches the policy bundle for a PLM-type WAFPolicy by reading the
1369	// referenced APPolicy's status and downloading the bundle from S3.
1370	func fetchPLMPolicyBundle(
1371		ctx context.Context,
1372		logger logr.Logger,
1373		wafPolicy *ngfAPIv1alpha1.WAFPolicy,
1374		policy *Policy,
1375		wafInput *WAFProcessingInput,
1376		output *WAFProcessingOutput,
1377	) {
1378		if wafInput.PLMFetcher == nil {
1379			cond := conditions.NewPolicyNotAcceptedPLMNotConfigured()
1380			policy.Conditions = append(policy.Conditions, cond)
1381			policy.Valid = false
1382			return
1383		}
1384	
1385		if wafPolicy.Spec.PolicyRef == nil || wafPolicy.Spec.PolicyRef.APPolicyRef == nil {
1386			return
1387		}
1388		ref := wafPolicy.Spec.PolicyRef.APPolicyRef
1389	
1390		targetNs := wafPolicy.Namespace
1391		if ref.Namespace != nil {
1392			targetNs = *ref.Namespace
1393		}
1394		nsName := types.NamespacedName{Namespace: targetNs, Name: ref.Name}
1395	
1396		// Check cross-namespace reference permission.
1397		if !validatePLMAPolicyReference(wafPolicy, policy, wafInput, nsName) {
1398			return
1399		}
1400	
1401		apPolicy, status, ok := getPLMAPPolicyStatus(policy, wafInput, nsName)
1402		if apPolicy != nil {
1403			output.ReferencedAPPolicies[nsName] = apPolicy
1404		}
1405		if !ok {
1406			return
1407		}
1408	
1409		policyNsName := types.NamespacedName{Namespace: wafPolicy.Namespace, Name: wafPolicy.Name}
1410		bundleKey := PLMPolicyBundleKey(policyNsName)
1411	
1412		creds, tlsCfg, err := plmFetchCredentials(wafInput)
1413		if err != nil {
1414			if prev, ok := handlePLMBundleFetchError(policy, wafInput.PreviousBundles, bundleKey, "policy bundle", err); ok {
1415				output.Bundles[bundleKey] = prev
1416			}
1417			return
1418		}
1419	
1420		data, err := wafInput.PLMFetcher.FetchBundle(
1421			ctx,
1422			status.Bundle.Location,
1423			status.Bundle.SHA256,
1424			creds,
1425			tlsCfg,
1426		)
1427		if err != nil {
1428			logger.Error(err, "Failed to fetch PLM policy bundle", "resource", wafPolicy.Name, "apPolicy", nsName)
1429			if prev, ok := handlePLMBundleFetchError(policy, wafInput.PreviousBundles, bundleKey, "policy bundle", err); ok {
1430				output.Bundles[bundleKey] = prev
1431			}
1432			return
1433		}
1434	
1435		bundleData := &WAFBundleData{Data: data, Checksum: status.Bundle.SHA256}
1436		output.Bundles[bundleKey] = bundleData
1437		policy.WAFState.Bundles[bundleKey] = bundleData
1438	}
1439	
1440	func validatePLMAPolicyReference(
1441		wafPolicy *ngfAPIv1alpha1.WAFPolicy,
1442		policy *Policy,
1443		wafInput *WAFProcessingInput,
1444		nsName types.NamespacedName,
1445	) bool {
1446		if nsName.Namespace == wafPolicy.Namespace || wafInput.RefGrantResolver == nil {
1447			return true
1448		}
1449	
1450		if wafInput.RefGrantResolver.refAllowed(toAPPolicy(nsName), fromWAFPolicy(wafPolicy.Namespace)) {
1451			return true
1452		}
1453	
1454		cond := conditions.NewPolicyRefsNotPermitted(
1455			fmt.Sprintf("cross-namespace reference to APPolicy %q not permitted by ReferenceGrant", nsName),
1456		)
1457		policy.Conditions = append(policy.Conditions, cond)
1458		policy.Valid = false
1459	
1460		return false
1461	}
1462	
1463	func getPLMAPPolicyStatus(
1464		policy *Policy,
1465		wafInput *WAFProcessingInput,
1466		nsName types.NamespacedName,
1467	) (*unstructured.Unstructured, *wafv1.APPolicyStatus, bool) {
1468		apPolicy, exists := wafInput.APPolicies[nsName]
1469		if !exists {
1470			cond := conditions.NewPolicyRefsNotResolved(
1471				fmt.Sprintf("APPolicy %q not found", nsName),
1472			)
1473			policy.Conditions = append(policy.Conditions, cond)
1474			policy.Valid = false
1475	
1476			return nil, nil, false
1477		}
1478	
1479		status, err := wafv1.ParseAPPolicyStatus(apPolicy)
1480		if err != nil {
1481			cond := conditions.NewPolicyRefsNotResolved(
1482				fmt.Sprintf("failed to parse APPolicy %q status: %v", nsName, err),
1483			)
1484			policy.Conditions = append(policy.Conditions, cond)
1485			policy.WAFState.BundlePending = true
1486	
1487			return apPolicy, nil, false
1488		}
1489	
1490		if status.Bundle == nil {
1491			cond := conditions.NewPolicyRefsNotResolved(
1492				fmt.Sprintf("APPolicy %q bundle is not ready (state: unknown)", nsName),
1493			)
1494			policy.Conditions = append(policy.Conditions, cond)
1495			policy.WAFState.BundlePending = true
1496	
1497			return apPolicy, nil, false
1498		}
1499	
1500		if status.Bundle.State == wafv1.BundleStateInvalid {
1501			cond := conditions.NewPolicyRefsNotResolved(
1502				fmt.Sprintf("APPolicy %q bundle is invalid (state: %s)", nsName, status.Bundle.State),
1503			)
1504			policy.Conditions = append(policy.Conditions, cond)
1505			policy.Valid = false
1506	
1507			return apPolicy, nil, false
1508		}
1509	
1510		if status.Bundle.State != wafv1.BundleStateReady {
1511			cond := conditions.NewPolicyRefsNotResolved(
1512				fmt.Sprintf("APPolicy %q bundle is not ready (state: %s)", nsName, status.Bundle.State),
1513			)
1514			policy.Conditions = append(policy.Conditions, cond)
1515			policy.WAFState.BundlePending = true
1516	
1517			return apPolicy, nil, false
1518		}
1519	
1520		return apPolicy, status, true
1521	}
1522	
1523	// fetchPLMSecurityLogBundles fetches log profile bundles for PLM-type WAFPolicy SecurityLog entries
1524	// that reference APLogConf CRDs.
1525	func fetchPLMSecurityLogBundles(
1526		ctx context.Context,
1527		logger logr.Logger,
1528		wafPolicy *ngfAPIv1alpha1.WAFPolicy,
1529		policy *Policy,
1530		wafInput *WAFProcessingInput,
1531		output *WAFProcessingOutput,
1532	) {
1533		policyNsName := types.NamespacedName{Namespace: wafPolicy.Namespace, Name: wafPolicy.Name}
1534	
1535		for _, secLog := range wafPolicy.Spec.SecurityLogs {
1536			if secLog.LogRef == nil || secLog.LogRef.APLogConfRef == nil {
1537				// DefaultProfile or other source — no PLM bundle to fetch.
1538				continue
1539			}
1540			ref := secLog.LogRef.APLogConfRef
1541	
1542			if wafInput.PLMFetcher == nil {
1543				// PLM not configured but we have an APLogConfRef — should not happen due to CEL,
1544				// but guard against it.
1545				logger.Error(
1546					fmt.Errorf("APLogConfRef set but PLM is not configured"),
1547					"Skipping PLM log bundle fetch",
1548					"resource", wafPolicy.Name,
1549					"apLogConfRef", ref.Name,
1550				)
1551				continue
1552			}
1553	
1554			fetchPLMSecurityLogBundle(
1555				ctx,
1556				logger,
1557				wafPolicy,
1558				policy,
1559				wafInput,
1560				output,
1561				policyNsName,
1562				ref,
1563			)
1564		}
1565	}
1566	
1567	func fetchPLMSecurityLogBundle(
1568		ctx context.Context,
1569		logger logr.Logger,
1570		wafPolicy *ngfAPIv1alpha1.WAFPolicy,
1571		policy *Policy,
1572		wafInput *WAFProcessingInput,
1573		output *WAFProcessingOutput,
1574		policyNsName types.NamespacedName,
1575		ref *ngfAPIv1alpha1.APLogConfReference,
1576	) {
1577		nsName := apLogConfRefNamespacedName(wafPolicy.Namespace, ref)
1578		if !validatePLMAPLogConfReference(wafPolicy, policy, wafInput, nsName) {
1579			return
1580		}
1581	
1582		if apLogConf, exists := wafInput.APLogConfs[nsName]; exists {
1583			output.ReferencedAPLogConfs[nsName] = apLogConf
1584		}
1585	
1586		bundleKey := PLMLogBundleKey(policyNsName, ref)
1587		if _, alreadyFetched := output.Bundles[bundleKey]; alreadyFetched {
1588			return
1589		}
1590	
1591		status, ok := getPLMAPLogConfStatus(policy, wafInput, nsName)
1592		if !ok {
1593			return
1594		}
1595	
1596		bundleData, ok := fetchPLMLogBundleData(
1597			ctx,
1598			logger,
1599			wafPolicy,
1600			policy,
1601			wafInput,
1602			nsName,
1603			bundleKey,
1604			status,
1605		)
1606		if !ok {
1607			return
1608		}
1609	
1610		output.Bundles[bundleKey] = bundleData
1611		policy.WAFState.Bundles[bundleKey] = bundleData
1612	}
1613	
1614	func apLogConfRefNamespacedName(
1615		defaultNamespace string,
1616		ref *ngfAPIv1alpha1.APLogConfReference,
1617	) types.NamespacedName {
1618		targetNs := defaultNamespace
1619		if ref.Namespace != nil {
1620			targetNs = *ref.Namespace
1621		}
1622	
1623		return types.NamespacedName{Namespace: targetNs, Name: ref.Name}
1624	}
1625	
1626	func validatePLMAPLogConfReference(
1627		wafPolicy *ngfAPIv1alpha1.WAFPolicy,
1628		policy *Policy,
1629		wafInput *WAFProcessingInput,
1630		nsName types.NamespacedName,
1631	) bool {
1632		if nsName.Namespace == wafPolicy.Namespace || wafInput.RefGrantResolver == nil {
1633			return true
1634		}
1635	
1636		if wafInput.RefGrantResolver.refAllowed(toAPLogConf(nsName), fromWAFPolicy(wafPolicy.Namespace)) {
1637			return true
1638		}
1639	
1640		cond := conditions.NewPolicyRefsNotPermitted(
1641			fmt.Sprintf("cross-namespace reference to APLogConf %q not permitted by ReferenceGrant", nsName),
1642		)
1643		policy.Conditions = append(policy.Conditions, cond)
1644		policy.Valid = false
1645	
1646		return false
1647	}
1648	
1649	func getPLMAPLogConfStatus(
1650		policy *Policy,
1651		wafInput *WAFProcessingInput,
1652		nsName types.NamespacedName,
1653	) (*wafv1.APLogConfStatus, bool) {
1654		apLogConf, exists := wafInput.APLogConfs[nsName]
1655		if !exists {
1656			cond := conditions.NewPolicyRefsNotResolved(
1657				fmt.Sprintf("APLogConf %q not found", nsName),
1658			)
1659			policy.Conditions = append(policy.Conditions, cond)
1660			policy.Valid = false
1661	
1662			return nil, false
1663		}
1664	
1665		status, err := wafv1.ParseAPLogConfStatus(apLogConf)
1666		if err != nil {
1667			cond := conditions.NewPolicyRefsNotResolved(
1668				fmt.Sprintf("failed to parse APLogConf %q status: %v", nsName, err),
1669			)
1670			policy.Conditions = append(policy.Conditions, cond)
1671			policy.WAFState.BundlePending = true
1672	
1673			return nil, false
1674		}
1675	
1676		if status.Bundle != nil && status.Bundle.State == wafv1.BundleStateReady {
1677			return status, true
1678		}
1679	
1680		if status.Bundle != nil && status.Bundle.State == wafv1.BundleStateInvalid {
1681			cond := conditions.NewPolicyRefsNotResolved(
1682				fmt.Sprintf("APLogConf %q bundle is invalid (state: %s)", nsName, status.Bundle.State),
1683			)
1684			policy.Conditions = append(policy.Conditions, cond)
1685			policy.Valid = false
1686	
1687			return nil, false
1688		}
1689	
1690		state := "unknown"
1691		if status.Bundle != nil {
1692			state = status.Bundle.State
1693		}
1694	
1695		cond := conditions.NewPolicyRefsNotResolved(
1696			fmt.Sprintf("APLogConf %q bundle is not ready (state: %s)", nsName, state),
1697		)
1698		policy.Conditions = append(policy.Conditions, cond)
1699		policy.WAFState.BundlePending = true
1700	
1701		return nil, false
1702	}
1703	
1704	func fetchPLMLogBundleData(
1705		ctx context.Context,
1706		logger logr.Logger,
1707		wafPolicy *ngfAPIv1alpha1.WAFPolicy,
1708		policy *Policy,
1709		wafInput *WAFProcessingInput,
1710		nsName types.NamespacedName,
1711		bundleKey WAFBundleKey,
1712		status *wafv1.APLogConfStatus,
1713	) (*WAFBundleData, bool) {
1714		creds, tlsCfg, err := plmFetchCredentials(wafInput)
1715		if err != nil {
1716			return handlePLMBundleFetchError(
1717				policy,
1718				wafInput.PreviousBundles,
1719				bundleKey,
1720				fmt.Sprintf("log bundle (APLogConf %s)", nsName),
1721				err,
1722			)
1723		}
1724	
1725		data, err := wafInput.PLMFetcher.FetchBundle(
1726			ctx,
1727			status.Bundle.Location,
1728			status.Bundle.SHA256,
1729			creds,
1730			tlsCfg,
1731		)
1732		if err == nil {
1733			return &WAFBundleData{Data: data, Checksum: status.Bundle.SHA256}, true
1734		}
1735	
1736		logger.Error(err, "Failed to fetch PLM log bundle", "resource", wafPolicy.Name, "apLogConf", nsName)
1737		return handlePLMBundleFetchError(
1738			policy,
1739			wafInput.PreviousBundles,
1740			bundleKey,
1741			fmt.Sprintf("log bundle (APLogConf %s)", nsName),
1742			err,
1743		)
1744	}
1745	
1746	func plmFetchCredentials(wafInput *WAFProcessingInput) (*s3fetch.Credentials, *s3fetch.TLSConfig, error) {
1747		if wafInput.PLMResolvedSecrets == nil {
1748			return nil, nil, nil
1749		}
1750	
1751		return wafInput.PLMResolvedSecrets.Credentials,
1752			wafInput.PLMResolvedSecrets.TLS,
1753			wafInput.PLMResolvedSecrets.ResolutionError
1754	}
1755	
1756	// handlePLMBundleFetchError handles a failed PLM bundle fetch by falling back to a previously fetched bundle
1757	// if one exists (stale-bundle warning), or marking the policy as pending if no previous bundle is available
1758	// (fail-closed). Returns the fallback bundle data and true if a previous bundle was used.
1759	func handlePLMBundleFetchError(
1760		policy *Policy,
1761		previousBundles map[WAFBundleKey]*WAFBundleData,
1762		bundleKey WAFBundleKey,
1763		bundleDescription string,
1764		err error,
1765	) (*WAFBundleData, bool) {
1766		if prev, ok := previousBundles[bundleKey]; ok {
1767			cond := conditions.NewPolicyProgrammedStaleBundleWarning(bundleDescription, err.Error())
1768			policy.Conditions = append(policy.Conditions, cond)
1769			policy.WAFState.Bundles[bundleKey] = prev
1770	
1771			return prev, true
1772		}
1773	
1774		cond := conditions.NewPolicyNotProgrammedBundlePending(err.Error())
1775		policy.Conditions = append(policy.Conditions, cond)
1776		policy.WAFState.BundlePending = true
1777	
1778		return nil, false
1779	}
1780	
1781	// resolvePLMSecrets resolves PLM S3 credentials and TLS config from cluster Secrets.
1782	// plmSecretNames maps each secret NamespacedName to its PLMRole(s).
1783	func resolvePLMSecrets(
1784		logger logr.Logger,
1785		clusterSecrets map[types.NamespacedName]*corev1.Secret,
1786		plmSecretNames map[types.NamespacedName][]PLMRole,
1787	) *PLMResolvedSecrets {
1788		if len(plmSecretNames) == 0 {
1789			return nil
1790		}
1791	
1792		result := &PLMResolvedSecrets{}
1793		resolutionErrors := make([]error, 0)
1794	
1795		for nsName, roles := range plmSecretNames {
1796			secret, ok := clusterSecrets[nsName]
1797			if !ok {
1798				err := fmt.Errorf("configured secret %q not found", nsName)
1799				logger.Error(
1800					err,
1801					"PLM secret is not available",
1802					"secret", nsName,
1803					"roles", roles,
1804				)
1805				resolutionErrors = append(resolutionErrors, err)
1806				continue
1807			}
1808	
1809			for _, role := range roles {
1810				switch role {
1811				case PLMRoleCredentials:
1812					accessKeyID, secretAccessKey, err := resolvePLMCredentials(secret)
1813					if err != nil {
1814						logger.Error(err, "PLM secret is invalid", "secret", nsName, "role", role)
1815						resolutionErrors = append(
1816							resolutionErrors,
1817							fmt.Errorf("configured %s secret %q is invalid: %w", role, nsName, err),
1818						)
1819						continue
1820					}
1821	
1822					result.Credentials = &s3fetch.Credentials{
1823						AccessKeyID:     accessKeyID,
1824						SecretAccessKey: secretAccessKey,
1825					}
1826				case PLMRoleCA:
1827					caData, err := resolvePLMCA(secret)
1828					if err != nil {
1829						logger.Error(err, "PLM secret is invalid", "secret", nsName, "role", role)
1830						resolutionErrors = append(
1831							resolutionErrors,
1832							fmt.Errorf("configured %s secret %q is invalid: %w", role, nsName, err),
1833						)
1834						continue
1835					}
1836	
1837					if result.TLS == nil {
1838						result.TLS = &s3fetch.TLSConfig{}
1839					}
1840					result.TLS.CAData = caData
1841				case PLMRoleClientSSL:
1842					certData, keyData, err := resolvePLMClientSSL(secret)
1843					if err != nil {
1844						logger.Error(err, "PLM secret is invalid", "secret", nsName, "role", role)
1845						resolutionErrors = append(
1846							resolutionErrors,
1847							fmt.Errorf("configured %s secret %q is invalid: %w", role, nsName, err),
1848						)
1849						continue
1850					}
1851	
1852					if result.TLS == nil {
1853						result.TLS = &s3fetch.TLSConfig{}
1854					}
1855					result.TLS.CertData = certData
1856					result.TLS.KeyData = keyData
1857				}
1858			}
1859		}
1860	
1861		if len(resolutionErrors) > 0 {
1862			result.ResolutionError = errors.Join(resolutionErrors...)
1863		}
1864	
1865		return result
1866	}
1867	
1868	func resolvePLMCredentials(secret *corev1.Secret) (string, string, error) {
1869		secretAccessKey, ok := secret.Data[secrets.PLMS3Secret]
1870		if !ok {
1871			return "", "", fmt.Errorf("missing expected key %q", secrets.PLMS3Secret)
1872		}
1873	
1874		trimmedSecretAccessKey := strings.TrimSpace(string(secretAccessKey))
1875		if trimmedSecretAccessKey == "" {
1876			return "", "", fmt.Errorf("key %q must not be empty", secrets.PLMS3Secret)
1877		}
1878	
1879		return plmDefaultAccessKeyID, trimmedSecretAccessKey, nil
1880	}
1881	
1882	func resolvePLMCA(secret *corev1.Secret) ([]byte, error) {
1883		// Only validate Secret shape here so we can log which Kubernetes Secret is misconfigured.
1884		// TLS content validation happens later in the S3 fetcher when the assembled TLSConfig is used.
1885		caData, ok := secret.Data[secrets.CAKey]
1886		if !ok {
1887			return nil, fmt.Errorf("missing expected key %q", secrets.CAKey)
1888		}
1889	
1890		return caData, nil
1891	}
1892	
1893	func resolvePLMClientSSL(secret *corev1.Secret) ([]byte, []byte, error) {
1894		// Only validate Secret shape here so we can log which Kubernetes Secret is misconfigured.
1895		// TLS content validation happens later in the S3 fetcher when the assembled TLSConfig is used.
1896		certData, ok := secret.Data[secrets.TLSCertKey]
1897		if !ok {
1898			return nil, nil, fmt.Errorf("missing expected key %q", secrets.TLSCertKey)
1899		}
1900	
1901		keyData, ok := secret.Data[secrets.TLSKeyKey]
1902		if !ok {
1903			return nil, nil, fmt.Errorf("missing expected key %q", secrets.TLSKeyKey)
1904		}
1905	
1906		return certData, keyData, nil
1907	}

34		ref gatewayv1.LocalPolicyTargetReference,
35		basePath *field.Path,
36		groups []gatewayv1.Group,

38	) error {

40			path := basePath.Child("group")

44				ref.Group,
45				groups,

50			path := basePath.Child("kind")
51	
52			return field.NotSupported(
53				path,
54				ref.Kind,
55				supportedKinds,
56			)
57		}
58	
59		return nil
60	}

71		// Timeout defines a timeout for reading client request body. The timeout is set only for a period between
72		// two successive read operations, not for the transmission of the whole request body.
73		// If a client does not transmit anything within this time, the request is terminated with the
74		// 408 (Request Time-out) error.
75		// Default: https://nginx.org/en/docs/http/ngx_http_core_module.html#client_body_timeout.
76		//
77		// +optional
78		Timeout *Duration `json:"timeout,omitempty"`
79	}
